response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, versatile engineer with genuine systems-programming instincts, but his profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, while the last decade has centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and data platforms. The job demands current, hands-on C++/STL depth plus industrial-cybersecurity practice (SDL, IEC 62443), MQTT/message-broker architectures, and industrial automation context — none of which appear on the resume. His low-latency DLang work shows transferable aptitude, but the gap on the role's non-negotiables is too wide to justify an interview slot for a C++-centric senior position.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad engineering range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with a strong testing and software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling)
Prior professional C/C++ contribution to NI LabWindows/CVI and C++ audio-systems thesis work, indicating baseline familiarity with the language
concerns: C++ exposure is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); C++ is absent from his own skills list, and recent work is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle, IEC 62443, or industrial-cybersecurity experience, and no MQTT, message-broker, or industrial-protocol background
Domain mismatch: career is web/product platforms, not industrial automation or IoT; cloud experience is AWS rather than the preferred Azure

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or SDL/IEC 62443 experience
Strong low-latency systems and concurrency background
